titlestmt.titleproper: Guide to the Daniel Webster Crofts Papers
titlestmt.author: Compiled by Martha Lund Smalley

conventiondeclaration.abbr: finding aid prepared according to local divinity library descriptive practices.

maintenanceevent.eventdescription: This finding aid was produced using ArchivesSpace on Friday December 20, 2019 at 23:02

did.unittitle: Daniel Webster Crofts Papers
did.abstract: Letters and writings document the life and work of Daniel Webster Crofts, an American missionary serving under the China Inland Mission, primarily in Guizhou Province, China from 1895 to 1944. Detailed letters written to the Crofts children describe the evangelization and literature distribution work of Crofts and his first wife Verna Hammarén Crofts and his second wife Mabel Harlow Crofts, as well as their way of life in a rural area beset by famine, and political disruption.

prefercite.head: Preferred Citation
prefercite.p: Daniel Webster Crofts Papers (RG 240), Special Collections, Yale Divinity School Library.

bioghist.head: Biographical / Historical

chronitemset.event: Daniel Webster Crofts born in Ohio, son of John Byron Crofts and Hannah Kane Crofts. The Crofts had eleven children, ten of whom survived to adulthood: sons Frank, Daniel, Will, Rob, Jim, Tom and daughters Jennie, Ada, Minnie, and Mary.
Graduated from Muskingum College
Graduated from Chicago Theological Seminary
While working as minister for a congregation in Peoria, Illinois, applied to become missionary of China Inland Mission.
Arrived in Shanghai, having left Tacoma, Washington in November, and stopping in Japan. Began language study.
Stationed in Laohekou, Hubei Province
Married Verna Hammarén, a missionary sponsored by the Free Church of Finland working under the direction of the China Inland Mission
Son John born in Shanghai where Verna had evacuated due to Boxer Rebellion. Daniel arrived in Shanghai in November.
After being refugees in Shanghai for several months, lived in Yangzhou and Zhenjiang before returning to Laohekou
Daughter Verna born in Hankou. Shortly thereafter the family went on furlough, first to Finland and then to the U.S.
Son Alfred born in Oakland, California. Shortly thereafter the family returned to China, arriving in Shanghai in September
Established new CIM mission in Zhenyuan, Guizhou Province. Crofts' missionary work involved itinerations through countryside to preach and sell books.
Daughter Saima born.
Crofts contributed reports and columns to the North-China Herald

Son John and daughter Verna traveled to boarding school at Chefoo School in Yantai, Shandong, accompanied by wife Verna and son Alfred. Verna died in Chefoo from dysentery and weak heart. Daniel traveled to Chefoo and spent winter with children before returning to Guizhou.
Saima joined her older siblings at Chefoo School, having earlier lived with a missionary family in Panghai following her mother's death.
Crofts worked with fellow missionary M. H. Hutton in Panghai, using it as a base to work with Black Miao people.
Escorted son John to U.S. to enroll him in college, returning to China in fall of 1918
Married Mabel Harlow, CIM missionary based in Chefoo. They returned to Guizhou, stationed in Zunyi, while two middle children went to U.S. and Saima remained at Chefoo.
Opened new mission station in Qianxi, in northwest Guizhou.
Furlough in U.S.

Following furlough, Daniel and Mabel Crofts initially stayed in Shanghai, due to political disturbances in the interior, but in July 1928 moved to Zhenjiang and from late 1928 were stationed in Qanxi. In 1935-1936 the approach of Communist troops caused them to evacuate from Qanxi to Sichuan Province.
Furlough in the U.S.
Returned to China and were stationed in Qianxi, Guizhou
Mabel Crofts died of typhoid
Daniel Crofts left China and after visiting various relatives settled in Florida
Crofts died

bioghist.p: For additional biographical information see book published by Crofts' grandson Daniel W. Crofts: Upstream Odyssey: An American in China, 1895-1944 (Norwalk, CT: Eastbridge, 2008).

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: This collection primarily consists of regular, detailed letters written by Daniel Webster Crofts or his second wife Mabel Harlow Crofts to the four Crofts children who from an early age were separated from their parents at boarding school in Chefoo, and then in the U.S. The original format letters in the collection are complemented by scans of some additional documents, as well as the detailed, scholarly biography of Crofts written by his grandson Daniel Webster Crofts, a copy of which is in the Divinity Library's cataloged collection. Series I, Correspondence, is primarily family correspondence with a few folders of non-family letters. Series II, Writings, contains clippings of articles written by Daniel Crofts for the North-China Herald. Series III, Biographical Documentation, contains a few documents regarding the Crofts family and Crofts' wives. Much more detailed information is available in the biography noted above.

accessrestrict.head: Conditions Governing Access
accessrestrict.p: The materials are open for research.

did.unittitle: Correspondence

CD containing PDF scans of letters and other documents contained in this collection plus some for which the originals are no longer available. Information regarding "Upstream Odyssey: An American in China, 1895-1944" by Daniel Wallace Crofts, grandson of Daniel Webster Crofts
Family correspondence
To extended family
Primarily from DWC to siblings, written from Chenyuan [Zhenyuan], Chefoo, Kiensi [Qianxi], Palatka
To children

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Letters to children are primarily from Daniel Crofts, with some from second wife Mabel.

did.unittitle: Letters to children at school in Chefoo from DWC in Panghai and Chenyuan and while on trip to U.S. in late 1917
Letters to children, some of whom are in the U.S. at school
Incl. re. trip to Finland and U.S. and return to China
Written from Shanghai while waiting for permission to return to Guizhou
Incl. re. daily life at Kiensi [Qianxi] station
Primarily from Kiensi; incl. re. trip escorting new missionaries to Chungking, building, medical work
Primarily from Kiensi; incl. re. civil war conditions; traveling to meetings
Primarily from Kiensi; incl. re. concerns for children's needs during Depression in U.S.
Primarily from Kiensi; incl. re. robberies, political unrest, country tour
Primarily from Kiensi; incl. re. month's visit to Sinchang, new outstation
Primarily from Kiensi; incl. re. country travels, Bible school
Primarily from Kiensi; Communist troubles; travel
Furlough in U.S., England, Finland - visiting family and friends
Furlough in U.S. and return to China
Re. return to mission work, with time spent in Tushan, Kweiyang, and Kiensi; repair to house in Kiensi
Re. mission work in Kiensi
Incl. re. time spent in Kopu [Gebu] teaching classes for church workers
Incl. re. daily life in Kiensi, opinions re. war in Europe
DWC distributing tracts and Bible; medical treatment of soldiers and others
Incl. re. attendance at conference in Kweiyang [Guiyang], high prices, dispensing medicines
Government motor school established in Kiensi, leading to large influx of soldiers; tract ministry
Incl. re. death of Mabel; DWC goes to Kweiyang, then Pingpa [Pingba] before return to Kiensi
DWC's daily life in Kiensi, teaching English to Chinese men, joined by missionary family
Making preparations to leave China; begins journey to U.S. via India
Arrives in California, traveling in U.S. and Canada
Continues travels, visiting friends and family
Settles in Jacksonville, Florida with sister and after her death in Palatka with daughter Saima and family
In Palatka, assisting with Saima and family with chicken business
In Palatka and travels west
In Palatka and traveling
General correspondence
Circular letters
Typed excerpts of circular letters and diary entries of Verna Hammarén, stationed in Chinkiang [Zhenjiang]
To/from others
Incl. to/from Wilkinson, Lagerquist, Solomon, Houghton, Cook
Letter from Robert Chang to Rev. Yong
Writings
News articles written by DWC while stationed in Kiensi [Qianxi], including regarding famine, opium, bandits
Biographical Documentation
Incl. re. Crofts family history, biographical sketch re. Verna Hammarén, death certificate of Catherine Mabel Harlow Crofts, lists of household items
Collected material: cards, clippings
